+# AI Dispatch Log
+
+- Timestamp: 2026-05-26T00:26:40.4501258+03:00
+- Dispatch: $Id
+- Issue: #192 - Add same-phase retry for read-only model reviews
+- Issue URL: https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE/issues/192
+- Branch: $Branch
+- Loop exit code: $LoopExit
+- Codex control verdict: $Verdict
+- Loop log: $LoopLog
+
+## Process Trace
+
+1. Queue selected the oldest open ai-dispatch issue.
+2. Queue labelled the issue ai-dispatch-running.
+3. Queue created branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-192.
+4. Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 ran Codex plan, Claude gate, Claude execute, and Codex control.
+5. Queue wrote this detailed log before staging, committing, merging, or pushing.
+6. If and only if exit code is 0 and Codex control verdict is pass, queue will fast-forward main and push origin/main.
+
+## Files Changed / Added / Deleted
+
+git status --short --untracked-files=all before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text

+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_2026-05-26_00-21-28+0300.md

+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.meta.json
+~~~
+
+git diff --name-status before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text

+~~~
+
+git diff --stat before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text
+Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | 75 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
+ 1 file changed, 71 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)
+~~~
+
+This log file is also added by the queue before publish:
+
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_00-26-40+0300.md
+
+## Generated Run Files
+
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/claude.ready.stderr.txt (0 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/claude.ready.envelope.json (2750 bytes)

+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/claude.execute.round0.stderr.txt (0 bytes)

+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/verification.round0.log (1081558 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/codex.prompt.md (1050 bytes)

+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/codex.control.round0.log (1442268 bytes)

+## Claude Marker Summary
+

+EXEC_PACKET: ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_2026-05-26_00-21-28+0300.md

+## Codex Control JSON

The canonical verification log corroborates EXEC's claim that the dispatch gate passed all 7 steps. Claude gate verdict was approve.","exit_code":0},{"command":"Get-Content -Path .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/claude.execute.round0.md","result":"Pass. EXEC_STATUS was executed and EXEC_PACKET points to the reviewed ISSUE-192 execution report.","exit_code":0}],"commit_readiness":"ready_for_publish","commands_run":["Get-Content -Path AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md","Get-Content -Path ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md","Get-Content -Path ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.md","Get-Content -Path ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_2026-05-26_00-21-28+0300.md","Get-Content -Path .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/verification.round0.log","git status --short --branch","git diff -- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1","git diff --name-only","git diff --check","Get-ChildItem -Path ai_handoffs -Filter 'ISSUE-192*' | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name","Get-Content -Path Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | Select-Object -Skip 80 -First 110","Get-Content -Path Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | Select-Object -Skip 500 -First 170","Get-Content -Path Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | Select-Object -Skip 700 -First 210","Get-Content -Path Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | Select-Object -Skip 1130 -First 220","Get-Content -Path Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | Select-Object -Skip 250 -First 250","Get-Content -Path Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | Select-Object -Skip 900 -First 180","Get-Content -Path ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_2026-05-26_00-21-28+0300.meta.json","Get-Content -Path .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/claude.plan_gate.rev0.md","Get-Content -Path .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/claude.execute.round0.md","Get-Content -Path .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-192/verification.round0.log -Tail 40"]}

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-192
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_00-21-28+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 — add same-phase retry wrapper for the two read-only model review phases (Claude plan gate, Codex control)
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.md — authoritative TASK packet for this dispatch
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_2026-05-26_00-21-28+0300.md — this execution report
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch. The orchestrator dispatch
+protocol (see `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`) does not produce a separate
+`_REVIEW_` packet between the Claude plan-gate review and the Executor
+execution; the in-loop Claude plan-gate review approved the TASK packet
+before this round began.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`:
+  - Introduced a script-scoped `$script:RetryableFailEnabled` flag (default
+    `$false`) and updated `Fail` so it `throw`s the message when the flag is
+    set, otherwise preserves the existing `[Console]::Error.WriteLine` + `exit
+    1` behavior. This lets a same-phase retry catch a Fail from inside the
+    eligible read-only model-call path without converting any unrelated Fail
+    into a retryable exception.
+  - Added `Invoke-WithSamePhaseRetry` helper (one function, ~45 lines)
+    that: enables the retry flag inside a `try`/`finally`, runs the supplied
+    `[scriptblock]$Action`, catches any failure thrown via the flag, deletes
+    the listed `CleanupPaths` so a partial first attempt cannot be mistaken
+    for the retry result, and re-runs the action exactly once. It prints
+    `[retry] ...` lines via `Write-Host` (information stream — does not
+    pollute the output pipeline that callers assign into variables). On
+    retry exhaustion the flag is cleared and `Fail` is re-invoked with the
+    final attempt's message so the original failure path — including the
+    Codex stall watchdog wording — is preserved.
+  - Wrapped the Claude plan-gate call site (inside the `for ($i = 0; ...
+    MaxPlanRevisions; ...)` loop) with `Invoke-WithSamePhaseRetry`, cleaning
+    `claude.plan_gate.rev{N}.md`, `claude.plan_gate.rev{N}.envelope.json`,
+    and `claude.plan_gate.rev{N}.stderr.txt` between attempts.
+  - Wrapped the Codex control call site (inside the `for ($round = 0; ...
+    MaxCorrectionRounds; ...)` loop) with `Invoke-WithSamePhaseRetry`,
+    cleaning `codex.control.round{N}.json` and `codex.control.round{N}.log`
+    between attempts.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added. The TASK packet `MUST NOT add new files` set forbids test
+  files, fixtures, and any source file beyond `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+  Behavior is verified through the PowerShell parser, `git diff --check`,
+  the canonical `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` gate, and static inspection of
+  the diff (see Verification Results below).
+
+### Docs
+
+- None changed. The TASK packet's `MUST NOT edit` set covers docs, ADRs,
+  status files, schemas, and queue/auto runner code.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Single file changed: `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`. The diff is bounded to:
+
+1. Top-of-script: declare `$script:RetryableFailEnabled = $false`, update
+   `Fail`, add `Invoke-WithSamePhaseRetry`.
+2. Plan loop: insert three local artifact-path variables for cleanup and
+   replace the direct `Invoke-ClaudePlanGate` call with the wrapped form.
+3. Execution loop: insert two local artifact-path variables for cleanup
+   and replace the direct `Invoke-CodexControl` call with the wrapped form.
+
+No other functions, parameters, schemas, or call sites were modified.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+  (`[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(...)`) →
+  exit 0, no errors emitted ("Parser OK").
+- `git diff --check` → exit 0, no whitespace issues.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0, final line `VERIFY OK: all 7
+  verification step(s) passed.` Steps: `cargo +nightly fmt --check`,
+  architecture lints, architecture-lint test suite, `cargo deny`, workspace
+  tests, workspace doctests, and `cargo bench --no-run` all green.
+- Static inspection of the diff confirms retry wrapping is limited to
+  `Invoke-ClaudePlanGate` and `Invoke-CodexControl`. No other phase
+  (`Invoke-PlanFill`, `Invoke-ClaudeExecute`, `Test-ClaudeCliReady`,
+  `Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit`, `Invoke-Verification`,
+  `Invoke-CorrectionPacket`, queue/auto runners) is wrapped.
+- Static inspection of the diff confirms semantic verdict handling is
+  unchanged: the `$gate.verdict` `approve` / `needs_changes` / `block`
+  branches and the `$finalControl.verdict` `pass` / `needs_changes` /
+  `block` branches, plus their `Fail` / CORRECTION_PACKET routing and the
+  `MaxPlanRevisions` / `MaxCorrectionRounds` exhaustion checks, are
+  byte-for-byte identical to the pre-change source.
+- Static inspection of the diff confirms the final Codex stall watchdog
+  wording is unchanged: the `Fail "codex exec stalled: no log growth for
+  ${CodexStallThresholdSec}s after first output. Killed process tree. See
+  $LogPath"` line in `Invoke-CodexPrompt` is untouched, and on retry
+  exhaustion the wrapper re-invokes `Fail` with the captured message so
+  the exact stderr text reaches the orchestrator's parent process.
+- `git diff --name-only` → only `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`. The dispatch's
+  own TASK packet + sidecar and this EXEC packet are the only other
+  artifacts present in `git status` (all within the TASK packet's allowed
+  surface).
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. Only
+`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` was edited; this dispatch's TASK packet, EXEC
+packet, and (after finalization) sidecar are the only handoff artifacts
+touched.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- The retry wrapper deletes the first-attempt artifacts before retrying
+  rather than renaming them to an `.attempt1.*` suffix. The TASK packet
+  lists deleting/replacing as the first acceptable approach and asks for a
+  "compact implementation"; deletion was chosen for simplicity. If the
+  Reviewer prefers preserving first-attempt logs for post-mortem
+  visibility, the wrapper can be expanded to rename instead — but that
+  would add accumulation across rounds.
+- The retry status output uses `Write-Host` rather than `Write-Output` so
+  the `[retry] ...` lines do not enter the success-output pipeline (which
+  would corrupt callers like `$gate = Invoke-WithSamePhaseRetry ...`).
+  This matches how `Fail` writes to `[Console]::Error` and how internal
+  helpers avoid `Write-Output`. The Reviewer may want to confirm
+  `Write-Host` visibility under the queue runner's log capture.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` (modified, uncommitted — the
+  protocol forbids the Executor from committing).
+- Untracked items:
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.md`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.meta.json`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_2026-05-26_00-21-28+0300.md`
+    (this packet; will gain a `.meta.json` sidecar after finalization)
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-192`
+- Last commit: `de68e9f chore(dispatch): queue read-only phase retry task`
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-192
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,305 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-192
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300
+REVISION: 0
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_CORRECT_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_CORRECT_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #192
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Add a same-phase retry for the two read-only model review phases in
+`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`: Claude plan-gate review and Codex control review.
+Each eligible phase may retry once after an infrastructure or model-call
+failure, so transient CLI/model failures do not abort an otherwise valid
+dispatch. The retry must be local to the failed read-only phase and must not
+change semantic verdict handling, correction loops, publish behavior, GitHub
+API behavior, schemas, or the Codex stall watchdog's final failure wording.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- This dispatch's own generated handoff artifacts only:
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_*.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_*.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_CORRECT_*.md`, and
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_CORRECT_*.meta.json`.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one. Do not manually create or edit unrelated dispatch logs.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `.ai/**`, including `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`,
+  `.ai/codex_control.schema.json`, `.ai/handoff.schema.json`, run-dir control
+  JSON schemas, dashboards, task-selection briefs, and JSONL trace schemas or
+  path policy
+- `ai_handoffs/**`, except this dispatch's own `ISSUE-192_TASK`,
+  `ISSUE-192_EXEC`, and `ISSUE-192_CORRECT` Markdown packets and matching
+  `.meta.json` sidecars listed above
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/**`, except this dispatch's own exact queue log artifact if
+  the queue runner creates one
+- Rust source, Cargo manifests, `Cargo.lock`, tests, fixtures, workflows,
+  docs, ADRs, status files, architecture-lint files, `.gitignore`, repository
+  metadata, sandbox worktrees, or any file outside the allowed source/artifact
+  surface
+
+### MAY add new files
+
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_TASK_2026-05-26_00-09-40+0300.meta.json`
+  sidecar.
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_*.md` execution
+  report and matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar.
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_CORRECT_*.md`
+  correction packet and matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-192_CORRECT_*.meta.json`
+  sidecar, only if a correction round is required by the orchestrator.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+
+- Any source file, test file, fixture, generated asset, manifest, lockfile,
+  dependency file, crate, workflow, schema, script, doc, ADR, plan, status
+  file, architecture-lint artifact, dashboard, `.gitignore` entry, or
+  repository metadata file.
+- Any handoff packet or sidecar for another dispatch.
+- Any `REVIEW` or `CLOSEOUT` packet for this orchestrator dispatch.
+- Any `ai_dispatch_logs/**` file other than this dispatch's own exact queue log
+  artifact if one is created by the queue runner.
+- Any durable JSONL trace file, dashboard, aggregation artifact, or local
+  runtime artifact intended for commit.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Add a small, loop-local retry mechanism in `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` that
+  retries only these read-only phases after infrastructure or model-call
+  failure:
+  - `Invoke-ClaudePlanGate`, which calls Claude with `--permission-mode plan`
+    and extracts `GATE_VERDICT`.
+  - `Invoke-CodexControl`, which calls Codex with the `read-only` sandbox and
+    writes the dispatch run-dir `codex.control.roundN.json` artifact.
+- Each eligible phase may make at most one same-phase retry after the initial
+  failed attempt. There must never be more than two total attempts for one
+  phase invocation.
+- A retry-eligible failure is a thrown or terminating failure from the model
+  call path for that phase, including timeout, stalled Codex output,
+  non-zero CLI exit, missing output, malformed/missing marker output, malformed
+  control JSON, or model-reported CLI error. The retry should re-run the same
+  phase with the same prompt inputs and round/revision number, not advance to
+  another phase.
+- Preserve the existing final failure messages after retry exhaustion. In
+  particular, the final Codex stall failure must still use the current wording:
+  `codex exec stalled: no log growth for ... after first output. Killed process
+  tree. See ...`.
+- Emit clear loop output for retry activity without changing any JSON schemas
+  or JSONL trace schema:
+  - when a same-phase retry is about to run,
+  - when a retry succeeds, and
+  - when the retry is exhausted and the original phase is failing.
+- Keep retry output in normal loop stdout/stderr only. Do not add structured
+  trace fields, sidecar fields, schema fields, persistent dashboards, or new
+  log formats.
+- Ensure retry attempts use distinct or safely overwritten phase artifacts so a
+  failed first attempt cannot be mistaken for the successful retry result.
+  Acceptable approaches include deleting/replacing the phase's previous output
+  before retry, using attempt-suffixed transient logs and promoting the final
+  result, or another compact implementation that leaves the existing public
+  artifact names understandable.
+- Preserve existing semantic verdict behavior:
+  - Claude plan-gate `approve` finalizes the task and proceeds.
+  - Claude plan-gate `needs_changes` consumes a plan revision and routes back
+    to Codex plan-fill without same-phase retry.
+  - Claude plan-gate `block` aborts without same-phase retry.
+  - Codex control `pass` ends successfully without same-phase retry.
+  - Codex control `needs_changes` writes a correction packet when correction
+    rounds remain, or fails with the existing exhausted-round behavior when
+    they do not, without same-phase retry.
+  - Codex control `block` aborts without same-phase retry.
+- Do not make any other phase retryable. In particular, do not add same-phase
+  retry to Codex plan-fill, Claude execution, Claude readiness probe, Codex
+  preflight audit, verification, correction packet generation, correction
+  execution, queue publish, queue relabel, issue creation, branch operations,
+  GitHub API calls, or any queue/auto runner behavior.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only source file changed is `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+- Claude plan-gate model-call failures are retried once in the same
+  plan-gate revision before the dispatch aborts.
+- Codex control model-call failures are retried once in the same control round
+  before the dispatch aborts.
+- A successful retry leaves normal downstream state intact: the approved
+  Claude gate can finalize the TASK, and a successful Codex control retry can
+  produce the same final control JSON/readiness output expected by the queue.
+- A retry success prints a human-readable loop message that identifies the
+  phase and attempt outcome.
+- Retry exhaustion prints a human-readable loop message that identifies the
+  phase and exhaustion, then preserves the current final `Fail` message from
+  the underlying failure path.
+- Codex stall watchdog behavior is unchanged: the watchdog still kills a
+  stalled Codex process using the existing no-log-growth logic, still returns
+  the same stalled/timed-out result internally, and after retry exhaustion the
+  final stderr wording remains the existing `codex exec stalled: no log growth
+  for ... after first output. Killed process tree. See ...` message.
+- `ModelTimeoutSec`, `VerifyTimeoutSec`, `CodexStallThresholdSec`, and
+  `MaxPlanRevisions` / `MaxCorrectionRounds` semantics are unchanged except
+  for the single same-phase retry around the two eligible read-only phases.
+- Claude `needs_changes`, Claude `approve`, Codex `pass`, and Codex
+  `needs_changes` are treated as semantic verdicts, not retryable failures.
+- Claude `block`, Codex `block`, blocked/failed Claude execution,
+  verification failure, verification timeout, exhausted plan revisions,
+  exhausted correction rounds, and task/correction packet validation failures
+  remain non-retryable unless they arise specifically as the eligible model
+  call failure being retried in the read-only phase.
+- No retry is added to Codex plan-fill, Codex correction generation, Claude
+  execution, Claude readiness probe, Codex preflight audit, verification, queue
+  publish, queue relabel, issue creation, branch operations, GitHub API calls,
+  or any script other than `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+- No `.ai/codex_control.schema.json`, handoff schema, JSONL trace schema,
+  packet template, queue log format, or GitHub label behavior changes.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is only for same-phase retry of Claude plan-gate and Codex
+  control read-only model review phases.
+- Do not alter the meaning of model verdicts. A semantic verdict is a
+  completed phase result, even when it blocks progress or requests changes.
+- Do not add retry policy knobs, environment variables, command-line
+  parameters, dashboards, health summaries, issue labels, docs, schemas,
+  tests, dependencies, or helper scripts.
+- Do not change or wrap the queue runner's existing retry policy. This is an
+  inner-loop same-phase retry, not an issue requeue or publish retry.
+- Do not change commit, push, publish, branch, label, issue selection,
+  autonomous scheduling, or GitHub API behavior.
+- Do not change the canonical verification gate or make verification failures
+  eligible for same-phase retry.
+- Do not change preflight audit behavior. If `Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit`
+  fails, it must still fail without same-phase retry.
+- Avoid broad exception handling that hides failures after the single retry.
+  After the retry is exhausted, the dispatch must fail loudly through the same
+  failure path and wording used today.
+

+

+- Static inspection of the final diff must confirm the final Codex stall

+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of
+the following occur:
+
+- The same-phase retry cannot be implemented by editing only
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` plus this dispatch's own generated artifacts.
+- Preserving the final Codex stall watchdog failure wording conflicts with the
+  retry implementation.
+- Implementing the retry would require changing `Invoke-WithTimeout` semantics
+  for non-eligible phases, changing timeout/stall parameter meanings, or
+  retrying any phase other than Claude plan-gate and Codex control.
+- The implementation would retry semantic verdicts such as Claude
+  `needs_changes`, Claude `approve`, Codex `pass`, Codex `needs_changes`, or
+  either model's `block` verdict.
+- The implementation would change Codex plan-fill, Claude execution, Claude
+  readiness probe, Codex preflight audit, verification, correction packet
+  generation, correction execution, queue publish, queue relabel, issue
+  creation, branch operations, GitHub API calls, Auto Loop behavior, JSONL
+  trace schema/path policy, Rust/Cargo behavior, docs, dashboards, workflows,
+  or any file outside the allowed surface.
+- The retry output cannot be emitted without adding schemas, trace fields,
+  persistent dashboards, or JSONL trace schema changes.
+- PowerShell parser validation, `git diff --check`, or
+  `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` fails for a reason that cannot be fixed inside
+  the allowed edit set.
+- Any edit outside the allowed source/artifact set appears. Do not clean or
+  revert unrelated user work; halt and report the deviation.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The target call sites are `Invoke-ClaudePlanGate` and `Invoke-CodexControl`.
+Both are read-only review phases from the dispatch design perspective: the
+Claude gate uses plan permission mode and must not edit files, while Codex
+control uses the `read-only` sandbox and writes only its run-dir review
+artifacts. A compact helper that invokes a scriptblock, prints retry messages,
+and rethrows the final caught exception after one retry is likely sufficient.
+
+Be careful with the current `Fail` helper: it writes to stderr and exits the
+PowerShell process. If a retry wrapper cannot catch a `Fail` from the current
+call stack, refactor only the eligible phase call path enough to let those two
+read-only phases signal retryable model-call failures without changing the
+public final failure text. Do not turn unrelated `Fail` calls into retryable
+exceptions.
+
+The original review-gate strings for GitHub issue #192 are preserved here for
+review:
+
+```text
+MUST edit only Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 plus this dispatch's own ai_handoffs and ai_dispatch_logs artifacts
+MUST add same-phase retry only for Claude plan-gate and Codex control read-only phases
+MUST retry each eligible read-only phase at most once after infrastructure or model-call failure
+MUST NOT retry semantic needs_changes block pass or approve verdicts
+MUST NOT retry Codex plan-fill Claude execute preflight verification correction generation correction execution queue publish queue relabel issue creation or GitHub API calls
+MUST preserve Codex stall watchdog behavior and final stall timeout wording when retries exhaust
+MUST emit loop output for same-phase retry attempts successes and exhaustion without adding schemas or JSONL trace schema changes
+MUST run PowerShell parser validation for Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1, git diff --check, and the canonical .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1 gate successfully
+```
+
+No prior Claude gate result exists for this revision.
